title: rosa woodsii var. macounii
intro: Rosa woodsii var. macounii, also known as the Macoun's woods rose or Macoun's rose, is a subspecies of wild rose native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The medicinal properties of Rosa woodsii var. macounii are primarily attributed to its ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of using this plant include treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as relieving symptoms of fever, colds, and flu. Rosa woodsii var. macounii can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
